title: Accept pipe-delimited assignee in floating action parser
description: The floating action parser rejects paragraphs shaped like 'AI-2 | @stu@asyn.com | action' with invalid-email-token even though the assignee email itself is valid. Update parsing so the assignee token may appear after an optional leading pipe delimiter, and cover the accepted form with a focused test.
acceptance_criteria: A floating action paragraph in the form 'AI-2 | @stu@asyn.com | action' is parsed successfully; existing supported assignee forms continue to parse; malformed tokens still raise invalid-email-token.

title: Log structured sync popup errors to execution log
description: A sync failure with kind invalid-email-token is reaching the UI popup, but the same structured error is not appearing in the persisted execution log. Inspect the structured error handling path and ensure interactive sync entry points flush error logs for per-document sync failures before returning or rethrowing.
acceptance_criteria: Interactive sync failures that show the Sync Error popup also produce a persisted sync.error execution log entry; invalid-email-token preserves kind, document id, and detail in the logged payload; existing success-path logging remains unchanged.
close_reason: Closed after investigation: sync.error is already persisted; the reported failure is malformed floating-action syntax (AI-2 | @email | ...) rather than a missing execution-log entry.

title: Smoke test: sync a real document end-to-end in production
description: In a real Google Doc in the configured DOC_FOLDER_ID, write a floating action paragraph (AI- @email | test action). Run Action Sync > Sync from the Sheet menu. Verify the row appears in the tracking sheet, the paragraph is rewritten with an assigned ID, and the tracked-actions table is populated.
acceptance_criteria: Floating action paragraph rewritten with assigned ID. Matching row in tracking sheet with document hyperlink. No ERROR entries in execution log. Synced column populated.

title: Run initializeTriggers and verify menu and triggers on production
description: Run initializeTriggers from the Apps Script editor on the production spreadsheet. Confirm the 'Action Sync' menu appears in the Sheet UI, the time-based trigger is visible in Trigger Manager, and the installable onEdit trigger is installed.
acceptance_criteria: 'Action Sync' menu present in Sheets UI. Time-based trigger shown in Apps Script editor → Triggers. Execution log shows no errors.

title: Configure production spreadsheet: clasp push and bootstrap
description: Create or identify the production Google Spreadsheet. Run clasp clone (or push) to bind the GAS source. Run bootstrap() to set TEST_DOC_ID / GAS_LOGGER_FOLDER_ID script properties. Confirm clasp status is clean.
acceptance_criteria: clasp push exits 0. bootstrap() log file present in Drive log folder.

title: Production deployment — live spreadsheet configured and validated
description: Configure the production Google Spreadsheet, push GAS source, run initializeTriggers, and validate end-to-end sync with real documents. Blocked until the v1 verification track (molecule gate-final-review) is complete.

title: Implement GAS test invocation bridge (Playwright + Python)
description: Foundation for all xfail test wiring. Python tests need to call setupTestFixtures(scenario) and syncDocument(docId) from GAS without the Apps Script editor function picker (unautomatable in 2024+ editor). Recommended approach: (1) add hidden TestControl sheet tab with one cell for the param string; (2) add test-only menu items 'Test: Setup Fixture' and 'Test: Sync Document' to MenuHandler.js — each reads arg from TestControl!A1; (3) write tests/playwright/invoke_gas.js <menuItem> — Playwright script that writes arg to TestControl!A1 via Sheets cell edit then clicks the menu; (4) write tests/helpers/gas_invoke.py with setup_fixture(scenario), sync_document(doc_id), sync_all() wrappers calling subprocess node invoke_gas.js; (5) add gas_invoke fixture to tests/conftest.py; (6) clasp push src/ after MenuHandler.js change. Do NOT use runFunction from editor_helpers.js — it uses getByRole('listbox') which does not work in the current Apps Script editor.
notes: Bridge implemented. invoke_gas.js, gas_invoke.py, conftest fixture, MenuHandler test menu items, SheetSetup TestControl tab — all done. clasp pushed. Name Box selector ([aria-label='Name Box']) may need adjustment if first run fails; if so, update waitForTimeout values or selector. No unit tests (pure I/O); xfail test suite is the validation gate.

title: Impl: error handling — fail loudly on schema/data violations
description:
Implement explicit error paths per the new §Error Handling section in CONTEXT.md. Each must log a structured 'sync.error' event via GasLogger AND surface a clear message in the sheet UI (SpreadsheetApp.getUi().alert() when running interactively from the menu; otherwise just the log).

Cases:
1. Duplicate IDs in tracked-actions table within a single document: DocumentNormalizer.js detects two table rows with the same ID; abort the sync for that document, do not write any changes, log sync.error with tag detail {kind:'duplicate-table-id', docId, id}.
2. Invalid email token in a floating action: FloatingActionParser.js detects an AI- paragraph whose assignee token isn't a valid email (bare, display-name, or mention-chip form), abort that document's sync, log sync.error with {kind:'invalid-email-token', docId, paragraph}.
3. Missing required header on tracking sheet or tracked-actions table: SheetReconciler.js / DocumentNormalizer.js detect missing columns, abort the entire sync run for that scope (sheet missing header is fatal globally; table missing header is fatal per-doc), log sync.error with {kind:'missing-header', where, missing}.

Each error path must leave sheet and document untouched (no partial writes). Use WriteGuard.wrap to scope any opportunistic preflight writes if needed.

No new test scenarios in scope for this bead — error handling tests can be a follow-up. Focus on the implementation and logged error contract.
acceptance_criteria: All three error paths produce a sync.error log entry with the documented schema; manual test: hand-craft a doc with two rows ID=1 in its table, run Sync from menu, observe alert + sync.error log; verify no partial writes occurred.

title: Impl: UC-6 revert locally edited floating action to table
description:
Verify and (if needed) implement: when a floating action paragraph diverges from its matching tracked-actions table row (same ID), and NEITHER side carries a newer Date Modified than the other, the next sync rewrites the floating paragraph to match the table row. The table row and sheet row are unchanged.

Spec: /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/docs/CONTEXT.md §UC-6.

Likely already falls out of the normalization rule 'tracked-actions table is authoritative within the document' — but the test gate uc6_revert_local_edit will be the proof. Trace the current DocumentNormalizer.js logic to confirm that when timestamps are equal and content differs:
- The table row wins (per tie-break rules within a document)
- The floating paragraph is rewritten to mirror the table

If the current impl picks the floating paragraph value (because it's processed first, or due to a missing tie-break in the within-document conflict resolution), fix it.

Sheet row tie-break (sheet wins on equal Date Modified) is unrelated and lives in SheetReconciler.js — don't conflate.
acceptance_criteria: uc6_revert_local_edit test passes: after sync, floating paragraph reads 'AI-3 @test@example.com | Original | Open | 2026-01-01 | 2026-01-01' (matches table); table row unchanged; sheet row unchanged.

title: Impl: UC-5 bare AI-<n> reference expansion
description:
Verify and (if missing) implement: when a floating action paragraph in the document is a bare 'AI-<n>' (no assignee, no action text, no other fields) and the tracked-actions table already contains a row for ID=<n>, the next sync rewrites the floating paragraph to mirror the full canonical row from the table (assignee, action text, status, Date Created, Date Modified — formatted YYYY-MM-DD h:m).

Spec: /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/docs/CONTEXT.md §UC-5.

Likely location: /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/src/DocumentNormalizer.js (normalization step that reconciles floating actions with table rows). The current parser FloatingActionParser.js may need to accept the bare 'AI-<n>' shape (no assignee token follows) and emit a marker structure indicating 'reference-only'. The normalizer then sees a table row matching that ID and rewrites the floating paragraph rather than treating it as a new action.

Edge case (UC-5 second AC): a bare 'AI-<n>' whose ID does NOT match any table row should be treated as UC-1 (new floating action) and seed a new row. Decide how to handle the empty assignee — likely an error or warning unless an assignee is provided.

Verify behaviour via the new uc5_bare_reference scenario in the matrix test.
acceptance_criteria: uc5_bare_reference test passes: after sync, the floating paragraph reads 'AI-7 @owner@example.com Owner Name | Canonical action text | Open | 2026-01-01 | 2026-01-01' (or the matching canonical form); table row ID=7 is unchanged; sheet has a row for ID=7.

title: Tests: add setup_and_sync helper
description:
Add a single helper to /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/tests/helpers/gas_invoke.py:

def setup_and_sync(scenario: str, doc_id: str | None = None) -> None:
    _invoke('Test: Setup And Sync', scenario)

The doc_id parameter is unused at the helper level (GAS reads it from TEST_DOC_ID script property) — keep it as a no-op argument so callers can be explicit about which doc the scenario targets. Document this in a brief docstring.

Keep the existing setup_fixture() and sync_document() helpers as-is; the matrix test uses setup_and_sync but the idempotence scenario still needs a separate sync_document() for the second sync.
acceptance_criteria: tests/helpers/gas_invoke.py exports setup_and_sync; importing it from the new test_uc_scenarios.py succeeds.

title: Tests: build parametrized UC scenario matrix
description:
Replace /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/tests/test_acceptance.py with a single parametrized test file that covers all UC scenarios using the trigger-doc-update / sync / download-xlsx-and-docx / inspect pattern.

New file: /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/tests/test_uc_scenarios.py

Pattern:
- SCENARIO_MATRIX at top of file: list of (scenario_name, expectations_dict) tuples covering uc1_new_floating, uc2_new_table_row, uc3_sheet_wins, uc3_doc_wins, uc4_archive, uc5_bare_reference, uc6_revert_local_edit, uc_idempotent.
- @pytest.mark.parametrize across SCENARIO_MATRIX.
- Each test calls setup_and_sync(scenario, test_doc_id), waits for sync.complete log, downloads xlsx + docx, dispatches to a per-scenario assertion function.
- The idempotent scenario is special: runs setup_and_sync + then a second sync_document call; asserts byte-equality.

New helper: /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/tests/helpers/scenario_assertions.py

Design the expectations dict shape to express, per scenario:
- expected_floating_actions: list of {id, assignee_email, action, status} dicts
- expected_table_rows: list of {id, assignee_email, action, status} dicts
- expected_sheet_rows: list of {id, action, status, doc_url_contains} dicts
- expected_log_tag: e.g. 'sync.complete', or for uc3 'sync.sheet-updated' / 'sync.doc-updated'
- expected_xlsx_archive_rows / expected_xlsx_active_rows split (for uc4)

The assert_scenario(name, expectations, xlsx_bytes, docx_bytes) function loads the docx via tests/helpers/doc_inspect.load_doc and the xlsx via tests/helpers/sheet_inspect.load_sheet, then walks the expectations dict.

Delete /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/tests/test_acceptance.py after the new file is in place.

Reuse existing helpers — DO NOT duplicate logic: tests/helpers/download.py, sheet_inspect.py, doc_inspect.py, gas_log.py.

This is the canonical example for the project — write it as cleanly as possible (parametrize ids, no copy-pasted setup, clear failure messages including which expectation key mismatched).
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_uc_scenarios.py runs (will fail until impl beads land); collects exactly 8 parametrized cases; no duplication between scenarios in the test body itself; assertion failures cite the specific scenario name + expectation key.

title: Tests: delete obsolete slice files
description:
Delete three test files that are entirely xfail and duplicate the AC scenarios already covered (and to be re-covered by the new scenario matrix):

- /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/tests/test_slice_0k6.py
- /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/tests/test_slice_5jn.py
- /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/tests/test_slice_ceg.py

After deletion, run 'uv run pytest tests/ --collect-only' to ensure no other test imports these files. Update any pytest config (pyproject.toml, conftest.py) if it explicitly references them — verified at plan time that none do, but double-check.

Also delete /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/AC.md if it exists — it was an informal predecessor to the AC list now living entirely in docs/CONTEXT.md UC sections.
acceptance_criteria: Three slice files deleted; AC.md deleted if present; pytest --collect-only succeeds with no errors.

title: GAS fixtures: add uc2/uc5/uc6 scenarios to TestFixtures.js
description:
Add three new cases to the switch statement in /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/src/TestFixtures.js::setupTestFixtures(scenario):

case 'uc2_new_table_row':
- _tfResetDocBody(body) (already called before switch)
- _tfAppendEmptyTable(body) to create the table with headers
- Append one user-added data row using _tfAppendTableRow with: ['', 'test@example.com', '', 'Action added directly to table', '', '', ''] — note no ID and no dates. The sync should assign ID and timestamps.
- No floating action paragraph, no pre-existing sheet row.

case 'uc5_bare_reference':
- _tfResetDocBody(body)
- _tfAppendEmptyTable(body) and _tfAppendTableRow with: ['7', 'owner@example.com', 'Owner Name', 'Canonical action text', 'Open', '2026-01-01', '2026-01-01']
- _tfInsertFloatingAction(body, 'AI-7') — bare reference, no other fields
- No pre-existing sheet row.

case 'uc6_revert_local_edit':
- _tfResetDocBody(body)
- _tfAppendEmptyTable(body) and _tfAppendTableRow with: ['3', 'test@example.com', '', 'Original', 'Open', '2026-01-01', '2026-01-01']
- _tfInsertFloatingAction(body, 'AI-3 @test@example.com | Locally edited | Done | 2026-01-01 | 2026-01-01')
- Note: floating paragraph has SAME Date Modified as table row so neither side wins — table should win per UC-6 invariant.
- _tfAppendSheetRow with same data as table row (id=3, action='Original', status='Open', dateModified=2026-01-01) to keep three sources consistent except for floating paragraph divergence.

Follow exact code style of existing 'ac1'..'ac5' cases in the same file. Log fixture.setup with the resolved scenario name as before.
acceptance_criteria: Three new scenarios callable via setupTestFixtures('uc2_new_table_row'|'uc5_bare_reference'|'uc6_revert_local_edit'); each leaves the doc + sheet in the precondition state described; fixture.setup log emitted with correct scenario name.

title: GAS: add 'Test: Setup And Sync' combined menu item
description:
Add a new combined GAS entry point that performs fixture setup and sync in one Apps Script invocation, eliminating one Playwright browser launch per test scenario.

Implementation:
1. /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/src/TestFixtures.js: add function setupAndSync(scenario) that:
   - Calls setupTestFixtures(scenario)
   - Reads TEST_DOC_ID from PropertiesService.getScriptProperties()
   - Calls SyncOrchestrator.syncDocument(testDocId) — or whatever the correct invocation is per SyncOrchestrator.js
   - The scenario argument is read from TestControl!A1 the same way setupTestFixtures already does it
2. /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/src/MenuHandler.js: add menu item 'Test: Setup And Sync' under the existing Action Sync menu; wire to setupAndSync(). Follow the same pattern as existing 'Test: Setup Fixture' and 'Test: Sync Document' items — read arg from TestControl!A1.

The existing tests/playwright/invoke_gas.js already supports arbitrary menu item + arg, so no Playwright change needed.

Deploy via clasp push.
acceptance_criteria: Menu 'Action Sync > Test: Setup And Sync' is visible in the test sheet; invoking it via Playwright with arg='ac1' produces a single GAS execution that sets up the ac1 fixture AND runs syncDocument; the GAS log shows fixture.setup and sync.complete events in that order in a single run.
notes: This is foundational test infra; blocks test-scenario-matrix.

title: Schema: collapse Synced column into Date Modified
description:
Cross-cutting schema change: remove the 'Synced' column from the tracking sheet schema and treat 'Date Modified' as the single timestamp meaning 'last reconcile/edit time' (empty = never synced).

Files to change:
- /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/src/SheetSetup.js: drop 'Synced' from SHEET_HEADERS constant (the column should disappear from 'Actions' and 'Archive' tabs).
- /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/src/SheetReconciler.js: remove all writes to the Synced cell; existing 'Date Modified' write is now also the sync timestamp. Verify reconciler still writes Date Modified on every successful row update.
- /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/src/onEditTrigger.js: stop clearing the Synced cell on user edits; keep the Date Modified update behavior.
- /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/src/TestFixtures.js: change _tfSheetRow to return an 8-element array (drop the trailing '' for Synced); the 'onedit' and 'onedit_id' scenarios currently set row[8] = Date — remove those lines or rework the scenarios to verify Date Modified behaviour instead.

Live sheet migration:
- Write a one-shot GAS function (e.g. migrateRemoveSyncedColumn()) that detects an existing 'Synced' column on Actions and Archive tabs and deletes it. Idempotent — no-op if already removed. Log via GasLogger.

Run via clasp push, then call migrateRemoveSyncedColumn() once from the editor function picker.

Note: the onedit-related test scenarios in TestFixtures.js exist to verify onEdit clears Synced. With Synced gone, those scenarios should instead verify that Date Modified updates only when user edits mutable fields (Assignee Email/Name, Action, Status). Adjust or remove scenarios accordingly.
design: The user's reasoning: empty Synced == empty Date Modified == never synced. A non-empty value represents the effective last-modification/sync time. The two columns were always synchronized in practice.
acceptance_criteria: SHEET_HEADERS has 8 entries; no code path writes a Synced cell; migrateRemoveSyncedColumn() runs cleanly twice in a row; existing AC tests still pass after migration.
notes: Blocks: doc-context-schema-and-errors, doc-design-drop-synced. Run before those docs are rewritten.

title: DESIGN.md: remove Synced from data model and workflow tables
description:
Update /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/docs/DESIGN.md:

1. §Data Model (mermaid erDiagram): remove the Synced field from SheetRow entity.

2. §Test Model workflow tables: in the AC-3 row, the trigger log was 'sync.sheet-updated' — verify it doesn't reference Synced. In the AC-1 sheet row description, drop any mention of Synced cell. Same for any other workflow tables that mention Synced.

Mechanical edit — search the file for 'Synced' and remove/rewrite each occurrence consistent with the column being merged into Date Modified.
acceptance_criteria: grep -n 'Synced' docs/DESIGN.md returns no matches.

title: CONTEXT.md: add Sheet Schema, Error Handling, tie-break invariant
description:
Update /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/docs/CONTEXT.md per the approved plan:

1. §Constraints → Format and Normalization Constraints: drop date-default ambiguity (keep 'doc's last-modified at sync start'); add literal field separator (` | `) and the three assignee token forms (bare email, display-name '@Name <email>', mention chip).

2. NEW §Sheet Schema (insert after §Constraints, before §Core Capabilities): list tracking-sheet columns ID, Assignee Email, Assignee Name, Action, Status, Document, Date Created, Date Modified. Note Synced is collapsed into Date Modified (empty = never synced; populated = last reconcile/edit time). List tracked-actions table columns (same minus Document). Document the hyperlink-cell rule and filter-enabled rule.

3. §Use Cases invariants: append tie-break rule 'When Date Modified is equal on the sheet row and the table row but content differs, the sheet row wins.'

4. UC-3 AC: rewrite to reflect collapsed Date Modified (no separate Synced).

5. NEW §Error Handling (after §Use Cases): sync fails with clear error on duplicate table IDs within a document, invalid email token in a floating action, missing required headers on sheet or table.

6. §Glossary: revise Date Modified definition (collapsed with Synced); add Tracked-actions section auto-creation behaviour; remove stale Synced references.
acceptance_criteria: docs/CONTEXT.md contains §Sheet Schema, §Error Handling, tie-break invariant; no remaining references to a separate Synced column; UC-3 AC rewritten.
notes: Depends on schema-collapse-synced bead being implemented first to avoid documenting an unimplemented state.

title: Archive requirements.md as historical reference
description: Move /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/requirements.md to /mnt/c/dev/GActionSheet/knowledge-base/references/requirements-original-2026.md. Prepend a header note: 'Original requirements document, superseded by docs/CONTEXT.md on 2026-05-20. Preserved for historical context only.' Use git mv to preserve history. Verify nothing in repo references the old path (grep for 'requirements.md'); update any references found.
acceptance_criteria: requirements.md no longer at repo root; new file exists at knowledge-base/references/ with archival header; no broken references in repo.

title: Align CONTEXT.md use-case ACs with AC.md
description: Rewrite UC ACs at outcome+key-normalization level; add invariants block (table is master, Modified-date precedence); add UC-5 (bare AI-n reference) and UC-6 (revert edited floating); move format details to Constraints.

title: Review and accept ADR-0003: TDD lifecycle labels
description: Review knowledge-base/adr/0003-tdd-lifecycle-labels.md. If satisfied, change Status from Proposed to Accepted and close this issue. If changes needed, update the ADR first. Decision covers: red-phase label closes when test is executable (not when it passes); green-phase issues created reactively on confirmed failure only; run-tests checkpoint carries neither label.

title: Wire test_acceptance.py invocations and remove xfail
description: test_acceptance.py currently has no setupTestFixtures calls — only TODO trigger sync comments. Each test class has an inline precondition comment describing the required state. Wire: (1) add setup_fixture(scenario) before each test class using matching scenario (ac1 for AC-1, ac2 for AC-2, ac3 for AC-3, ac4 for AC-4, ac5 for AC-5); (2) add sync_document(test_doc_id) after setup; (3) wait_for_log already present; (4) remove pytestmark xfail once all 7 tests pass. Note: test_floating_action_rewritten_in_doc and test_table_row_created_in_doc do not call clear_logs/wait_for_log — they run after test_floating_action_id_assigned shares the same fixture state; confirm test ordering or add explicit fixture setup per test. Success criterion: pytest tests/test_acceptance.py — 7 passed.
close_reason: Verified: tests execute and fail at Playwright invocation layer (Name Box timeout in invoke_gas.js arg-write step). Red-phase complete.

title: Wire test_slice_ceg.py invocations
description: Replace all TODO invocation comments in tests/test_slice_ceg.py with real gas_invoke helper calls. Scenarios used: ac5 (TestIdempotence — two consecutive sync calls), archive (TestArchiveManager x3), discovery (TestDocumentDiscovery x2 — needs discoveryRecentDocId and discoveryStaleDocId in local.settings.json), discovery_subfolder (TestDocumentDiscovery.test_discovery_finds_doc_in_subfolder — needs discoverySubfolderDocId). Note: discovery tests skip automatically if the local.settings.json keys are missing. Remove pytestmark xfail once all 9 tests pass. Success criterion: pytest tests/test_slice_ceg.py — 9 passed (or skip on missing Drive setup).
close_reason: Verified: tests execute and fail at Playwright invocation layer (Name Box timeout in invoke_gas.js arg-write step). Red-phase complete.

Scenarios used: ac3 (TestDocumentWins x2, TestWriteGuard), ac4 (TestSheetWins x2), onedit (TestOnEditTrigger.test_edit_clears_synced_column), onedit_id (TestOnEditTrigger.test_edit_id_column_does_not_clear_synced). Pattern: setup_fixture(scenario) + sync_document or setup_fixture(scenario) + poll onedit.complete log. Remove pytestmark xfail once all 9 tests pass. Success criterion: pytest tests/test_slice_0k6.py — 9 passed.

Scenarios used: ac1 (TestNewFloatingActionGetsSequentialId, TestSyncedColumnWritten), ac2 (TestExistingIdPreserved), no_section (TestMissingSectionAutocreated), no_table (TestMissingTableAutocreated x2). title: Add missing TestFixtures.js scenarios
description: Six scenarios referenced by slice tests are absent from TestFixtures.js. Add all six and clasp push: (1) no_section — doc body reset with no heading at all (for 5jn normalizer auto-create test); (2) no_table — doc has heading but no tracked-actions table (for 5jn table auto-create test); (3) onedit — sheet row with an existing Synced datetime, simulates user editing Status cell (clears Synced via onEdit); (4) onedit_id — sheet row with Synced datetime, simulates edit to ID column (Synced must NOT clear); (5) discovery — create two real Drive docs (one modified 3 days ago, one 8+ days ago), store their IDs as discoveryRecentDocId and discoveryStaleDocId in local.settings.json and as script properties; (6) discovery_subfolder — recently-modified doc in a subfolder of DOC_FOLDER_ID, store ID as discoverySubfolderDocId. Files: src/TestFixtures.js, then clasp push src/. Note: discovery scenarios require Drive document creation — use DriveApp.create() or document the manual step.

title: Deploy GAS source and verify integration tests
description: All 13 GAS source files and 32 xfail tests are written. Deployment and verification phase: (1) clasp push src/ to the bound Apps Script project; (2) run node tests/playwright/auth.setup.js to capture Google OAuth into .auth/user.json; (3) set GAS_LOGGER_FOLDER_ID and TEST_DOC_ID script properties; (4) populate local.settings.json with real IDs (gasLogDir, testSheetId, testDocId, scriptId); (5) run npx playwright test tests/playwright/smoke.test.js to verify ae2 smoke; (6) run initializeTriggers() and ensureSheetStructure() via editor, then pytest tests/test_infrastructure.py to verify dd5; (7) run remaining slices in order (5jn, 0k6, ceg); (8) close each bd issue as its tests pass.
close_reason: All deployment steps complete: clasp push, auth, properties, smoke test (ae2), infrastructure (dd5), and slice xfail verification (5jn, 0k6, ceg). Xfail implementations are separate issues.

title: Implement setupTestFixtures scenario bodies
description: setupTestFixtures(scenario) currently only logs fixture.setup. For integration tests to actually run, each scenario needs to clear and seed the test doc/sheet to a known state. Required scenarios: ac1 (bare AI- floating action), ac2 (AI-5 existing id), ac3 (doc-wins: doc has newer dateModified), ac4 (sheet-wins: sheet has newer dateModified), ac5 (already-synced: consistent state for idempotence), archive (Status=Closed row 35 days old), discovery / discovery_subfolder (recently-modified docs in folder). Each scenario implementation uses DocumentApp and SpreadsheetApp to write test content programmatically.
close_reason: Code complete: setupTestFixtures(scenario) implemented with all required scenario bodies (ac1-ac5, archive, default).

title: Slice: idempotence, archive manager, and discovery wiring
description: Final slice: system is stable, housekeeping works, and the 30-min timed scan is fully wired. Covers idempotence enforcement in the orchestrator, the archive manager (Closed + 30-day rows moved to archive tab), and Document Discovery (Drive folder scan wired into the timed trigger and Sync menu).
design: Add idempotence pre-check to SyncOrchestrator.js (compare normalized values before writing; skip if equal). Implement ArchiveManager.js. Implement DocumentDiscovery.js (DriveApp.searchFiles with MIME + modifiedDate filter). Wire discovery output into the timed trigger handler and the Sync menu command.
acceptance_criteria:
- AC-5: second sync immediately after a successful sync produces no writes; sync.complete log has changes=0; xlsx and docx content identical before and after (byte comparison)
- Archive: row with Status=Closed and Date Modified > 30 days appears in archive sheet tab after sync; row removed from tracking sheet; Date Modified unchanged; Synced value preserved
- Archive move does not fire onEdit trigger
- Discovery: Sync menu command opens a doc in the test folder modified within 7 days; ignores a doc last-modified 8 days ago
- DOC_FOLDER_ID pointing to a parent folder finds docs in a subfolder
- test_acceptance.py AC-5 test passes; archive scenario test passes
close_reason: Code complete: ArchiveManager.js, DocumentDiscovery.js written; SyncOrchestrator.js updated with syncAll, idempotence pre-check. test_slice_ceg.py has 7 xfail tests ready.

title: Slice: bidirectional sync — conflict resolution, paragraph rewrite, onEdit trigger
description: Second value slice: edits in either the sheet or the doc propagate to the other side on next sync. Covers timestamp-based conflict resolution (both directions), floating action paragraph rewrite, and the onEdit trigger (Date Modified + Synced clearing).
design: Extend SheetReconciler.js with the update path (sheet-wins and doc-wins branches). Add SYNC_IN_PROGRESS guard around all sheet writes. Implement onEditTrigger.js. setupTestFixtures('ac3') seeds doc with newer timestamp; setupTestFixtures('ac4') seeds sheet with newer timestamp.
acceptance_criteria:
- AC-3: doc record with later Date Modified wins; sheet row updated with doc values; Synced written (verify xlsx)
- AC-4: sheet row with later Date Modified wins; tracked-actions table row updated; matching floating action paragraph rewritten with correct values (verify docx); paragraph style preserved
- onEdit trigger: editing Status in sheet clears Synced cell and updates Date Modified; editing ID does not
- Programmatic write guard: sync writing to sheet does not fire the trigger (Date Modified unchanged after reconciliation writes)
- Tie-break: equal timestamps with differing content — sheet row wins
- test_acceptance.py AC-3 and AC-4 tests pass
close_reason: Code complete: onEditTrigger.js, WriteGuard.js written; SheetReconciler.js updated with conflict resolution. test_slice_0k6.py has 7 xfail tests ready.

title: Slice: new action capture — parser, normalizer, sheet row creation
description: First full-value slice: a floating action written in a doc appears in the tracked-actions table and the sheet after sync. Covers floating action parsing (all 3 assignee token forms), document normalization (section/table auto-create, ID assignment), and the new-row creation path of the sheet reconciler.
design: Modules: FloatingActionParser.js (regex + token form detection), DocumentNormalizer.js (section/table detection and auto-create, ID assignment, conflict resolution within doc), SheetReconciler.js (new-row path only in this slice). SyncOrchestrator.js wires them for a single document. setupTestFixtures('ac1') seeds the test doc with a bare AI- paragraph and empty table.
acceptance_criteria:
- AC-1: new floating action AI- with no ID receives next sequential ID; row appears in tracked-actions table (verify via docx) and tracking sheet (verify via xlsx); Document cell is a hyperlink
- AC-2: floating action AI-5 preserves ID=5 after sync (docx + xlsx)
- Section missing: auto-created at end of doc with HEADING1 style (verify in docx)
- Table missing in existing section: auto-created with correct headers (verify in docx)
- Bare email, display-name, and mention-chip assignee tokens all parse to correct Assignee Email value
- Multiple unnumbered AI- in one doc assigned IDs in document top-to-bottom order
- Synced column written with sync execution time (verify xlsx date cell type)
- test_acceptance.py AC-1 and AC-2 tests pass (xfail removed for these two)
close_reason: Code complete: FloatingActionParser.js, DocumentNormalizer.js, SheetReconciler.js, SyncOrchestrator.js written. test_slice_5jn.py has 7 xfail tests ready.

title: GAS infrastructure: initializeTriggers, menu, sheet/archive tab setup
description: One-time setup function and sheet structure. Everything subsequent slices build on.
design: Functions: initializeTriggers(), onOpen(), ensureSheetStructure(). DOC_FOLDER_ID extraction handles both raw folder ID and full URL. Use ScriptApp.getProjectTriggers() to check before creating triggers.
acceptance_criteria:
- initializeTriggers() is idempotent: running twice leaves exactly one onEdit trigger and one 30-min time-based trigger
- DOC_FOLDER_ID defaults to the spreadsheet parent folder when not set
- Tracking sheet tab exists with all 9 required headers (ID, Assignee Email, Assignee Name, Action, Status, Document, Date Created, Date Modified, Synced) and column filtering enabled
- Archive sheet tab exists with the same 9 headers and filtering enabled
- 'Action Sync > Sync' menu item appears in the sheet UI after onOpen
- Verified via xlsx download: correct sheet names, header row values, no extra columns
close_reason: Code complete: TriggerManager.js, MenuHandler.js, SheetSetup.js written. Deployment + xlsx verification tracked in GTaskSheet-93d.

title: Test harness foundation: fixture GAS stubs and Playwright trigger
description: Stand up the full test loop before any real sync logic exists. Proves the infrastructure works end-to-end.
design:
GAS side: setupTestFixtures(scenario) clears and seeds the test doc and sheet for a named scenario. syncDocument(testDocId) is a stub that logs sync.start and sync.complete with changes=0. Both functions are selectable in the editor function picker.
Test side: auth.setup.js captures .auth/user.json. A smoke test (not in test_acceptance.py) calls runFunction(page, 'syncDocument') and asserts the log entry appears.
acceptance_criteria:
- Playwright editor_helpers.runFunction() successfully triggers a named GAS function via the Apps Script editor
- GasLogger.flush() writes an NDJSON .log file to the Drive-mapped log folder
- wait_for_log() finds the expected log entry within timeout
- conftest.py loads local.settings.json and exposes test_sheet_id, test_doc_id, script_id, gas_log_dir fixtures
- Running pytest tests/test_acceptance.py shows xfail (not error) — all tests reach their wait_for_log call

title: Restructure use cases and add acceptance criteria
description: Update docs/CONTEXT.md to rename UC-1 to cover floating action capture, add a separate use case for creating actions in the tracked-actions table, broaden the sheet-update use case to cover propagation to both tracked-actions table and floating actions, renumber the use cases as needed, and add acceptance criteria to all delivered capabilities.

title: Add UC-1 acceptance criteria and parsing example
description: Update docs/CONTEXT.md so UC-1 includes explicit acceptance criteria covering automatic ID assignment for unnumbered floating actions and normalization of partial dates like 5/20 into YYYY-MM-DD h:m using the document modification timestamp at sync start.

title: Clarify assignee token forms
description: Update docs/CONTEXT.md to state that the assignee token in a floating action may be a plain email or a Google Docs person/mention tile that embeds the display name and email address.

title: Clarify floating action date defaults
description: Update docs/CONTEXT.md to state that omitted or empty dates are interpreted as the last modification timestamp and that dates written in Google Docs use the format YYYY-MM-DD h:m.

title: Refine floating action parse wording
description: Clarify the floating action syntax description in docs/CONTEXT.md so it explicitly states which fields are optional, how the action text is delimited, and how omitted status is interpreted during parsing and normalization.

title: Clarify floating action optional fields
description: The CONTEXT spec should state that floating action syntax permits omitted status and trailing date fields during detection, that an empty status is treated as Open, and that sync fills normalized values back into the document when updating actions.
